expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: SKILL.md, README.md, reference.md, and AGENTS docs stay aligned
expectations.evidence: The output explicitly acknowledges the case focus but cannot cover the full set because AGENTS docs are missing from evidence: execution_notes.md: "AGENTS docs not present in provided evidence, so alignment with AGENTS documentation cannot be verified" and result.md: "AGENTS docs (not included in provided evidence)" / "alignment with AGENTS docs cannot be demonstrated from evidence." Therefore the expectation that the case focus is explicitly covered (including AGENTS alignment) is not met.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase docs
expectations.evidence: The output is consistent with the primary phase (docs) by evaluating cross-document phase-contract alignment and stating it is limited to provided evidence. result.md: "Primary phase under test: docs." It performs a docs alignment check across SKILL.md/README.md/reference.md and reports a coherent verdict with an advisory mismatch: "Status: Aligned on core orchestrator contract (no contradictions found in provided evidence)." It also flags a potential drift point rather than contradicting phase docs: "README claims an output that is not described in SKILL.md / reference.md."
